data.body: 9f25 GROUNDING CONFIRMED (corpus present): the code-review corpus IS in the local store — 963 code_review_result_v1 REVIEW_RESULT sidecar files under .tickets-tracker/<ticket>/<ts>-<uuid>-REVIEW_RESULT.json. Structure: the event file has top-level {event_type,data,timestamp,uuid,author,env_id}; the PAYLOAD is event['data'] (NOT 'payload'), with keys {schema:'code_review_result_v1', impact_model_version:'code-v2', verdict, blocking[], advisory[], coaching, coverage, change_fingerprint, change_id, revision, ticket_id, ...}. Each finding in blocking/advisory carries: criteria, decision, validity, impact, PRIORITY (=validity*impact), norm_id, evidence, finding, tier, severity, location, suggested_fix. So derive_code_review_threshold.py reads event['data']['blocking'|'advisory'][*]['priority'] directly (dedup by norm_id), adjudicates block-worthy vs not, computes precision(block-worthy|priority>=t). NEXT (9f25 impl): write the script + a tests/ unit test over a synthetic fixture (make-test coverage) + run it over the real 963-record corpus to emit the committed adjudication artifact + recommendation doc. My earlier '0 records' reading was a walk bug (events are per-file dicts under 'data', not a list). Corpus IS runnable here.
